messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project and repository",
      "description": "Create a new project folder, run npm init, and initialize a git repository. Add a basic README describing the app purpose.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder exists with package.json, README, and git initialized (git status shows repository)."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install required dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and dev tooling including tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, and a dev server tool (e.g., nodemon). Add scripts to package.json for dev and build.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "npm packages installed and package.json contains scripts for start, dev, and build. Node can require express without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Initialize Tailwind config and PostCSS, create input CSS file with Tailwind directives, and configure output build step for static assets.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind config and PostCSS files exist and running the build script produces a compiled CSS file that contains Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Create Express server to serve static frontend",
      "description": "Implement an Express app (e.g., app.js) that serves the compiled frontend assets from a public/static directory and starts on a configurable port.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies",
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts and responds to HTTP requests for static files (index.html, compiled CSS, JS)."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Build Hello World UI with Tailwind",
      "description": "Create the frontend page (HTML or template) containing the \"Hello World\" text element and UI controls (e.g., color inputs or preset buttons) styled using Tailwind classes.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "high",
      "successCriteria": "index.html loads in browser and displays the Hello World text plus visible color controls, and Tailwind styles are applied."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-live-update-js",
      "title": "Add frontend JavaScript for live color updates",
      "description": "Write frontend JS that listens to color control changes and updates the Hello World text color and background in real time without page reload.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Changing controls updates the text color/background immediately in the UI and changes persist until manually changed."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-persistence-api",
      "title": "Optional: Add API and persistence for color preferences",
      "description": "Implement a simple Express API to save and load the selected colors (e.g., JSON file or in-memory). Wire frontend to fetch saved preferences on load and save on change.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "create-express-server",
        "implement-live-update-js"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "User preferences are stored and reloaded: after saving a color, a page reload restores the chosen colors from the API."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down feature-driven: bootstrap project, install/configure tooling, implement backend server, build frontend UI, add interactive behavior, then optional persistence enhancement."
}
